hook: IRNA English and Breitbart — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Strait of Hormuz / US-Iran deal. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 32 buckets, 54 articles, 6 distinct frames in today's coverage.

Frame: ECONOMIC (10 buckets). "100 million barrels lost every week Strait of Hormuz is shut, Saudi Aramco CEO says

Oil prices continued to climb on Tuesday, as US Presid…" — AFP / France 24 EN

Frame: SECURITY_DEFENSE (9 buckets). "Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
US President Donald Trump has said the month-long ceasefire between the US and Iran…" — BBC World

Frame: POLITICAL (5 buckets). "Iran war adds tension to Trump-Xi summit in Beijing

U.S. President Donald Trump is heading back to China, but this time, the Iran war rais…" — Globe and Mail

Frame: CAPACITY_RESOURCES (5 buckets). "UN: Fertiliser disruption due to Hormuz crises may spark humanitarian emergency

UN: Fertiliser disruption due to Hormuz crises may spark h…" — Middle East Eye

The paradox: Iranian state media (relaying Al Jazeera analysis) and a senior US Democratic senator both concluded the US cannot militarily resolve the Hormuz closure — Tehran framing this as a strategic defeat, Murphy as pragmatic exit strategy — converging on the same withdrawa…

Silence as data: `south_korea` didn't cover this — they covered Both articles covered the domestic Yoon Suk-yeol trial and K-pop content respectively — zero Hormuz coverage despite South Korea being one of Asia's largest crude importers through the Strait. instead.
**Times of India** (india): Introduced the 'NACHO' (Not A Chance Hormuz Opens) market acronym replacing 'TACO' — signals that investors have abandoned hopes for a quick diplomatic resolution.
